headline: Google delays Gemini 3.5 Pro launch to July amid agent expansion
body: Google pushed back the release of Gemini 3.5 Pro to July while simultaneously advancing its AI agent capabilities through new partnerships and features like computer use integration. For healthcare AI implementations, this delay signals continued vendor uncertainty around production-ready enterprise models, forcing health systems to reassess timeline assumptions in their GenAI roadmaps. The parallel focus on agent functionality and third-party integrations (Google Sheets, ServiceNow) indicates Google is positioning Gemini for workflow automation across clinical and administrative processes rather than raw model performance. Healthcare organizations evaluating Google Cloud for EHR integration or value-based care analytics should treat July as a reset point for pilot timelines and capability expectations.
